# awsprof - AWS profile switcher CLI

`awsprof` is a command-line tool (binary `awsprof` / `awsprof.exe`) for picking
an AWS profile to log in as, from the list of profiles available on the machine.
It reads the standard AWS shared config files, resolves and (if needed) logs in
via SSO device authorization, and sets `AWS_PROFILE` in the current shell.

## Required setup: shell hook

Activation only works if a shell hook has been installed (once) so that the
export line `awsprof` prints on success gets `eval`'d into the current shell.
Without the hook, invoking the raw binary cannot change your shell's
environment - it only prints the line.

- bash: add to `~/.bashrc`: `eval "$(awsprof shell-init bash)"`
- zsh: add to `~/.zshrc`: `eval "$(awsprof shell-init zsh)"`
- fish: add to `~/.config/fish/config.fish`: `awsprof shell-init fish | source`
- PowerShell: add to `$PROFILE`: `awsprof shell-init powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression`

## Installation

```bash
# macOS / Linux via Homebrew (once the first release is cut)
brew tap payfacto/tap
brew install payfacto/tap/awsprof

# Pre-built binaries (Linux/macOS/Windows, amd64+arm64)
# Download an archive from https://github.com/payfacto/awsprof-cli/releases
# and place the `awsprof` binary on your PATH.

# Go developers
go install github.com/payfacto/awsprof-cli@latest

# Or build locally
go build -o awsprof .
```

## Profile discovery

Profiles come from the standard AWS locations:

- `~/.aws/config` - `[profile NAME]` and `[sso-session NAME]` sections
- `~/.aws/credentials` - `[NAME]` sections
- Overrides: `AWS_CONFIG_FILE`, `AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE`

`awsprof` reads these files. It does not write long-lived credentials into a
config of its own.

## Commands

```
awsprof                                         # interactive picker -> login if needed -> activate
awsprof <profile>                               # resolve (exact, then configured prefix) -> login if needed -> activate
awsprof list [--plain]                          # list profiles; marks the active one; --plain = bare names
awsprof whoami                                  # print current identity (AWS_PROFILE, Account, ARN); no switch
awsprof shell-init <bash|zsh|fish|powershell>   # emit the shell hook (see Required setup above)
awsprof use <profile>                           # hidden alias of `awsprof <profile>`
awsprof --version                               # print version
awsprof --help                                  # help
```

Persistent flag: `--shell` (default `bash`) selects export syntax; the
installed shell hook passes this automatically.

## stdout/stderr contract

On a successful activation, `awsprof` writes exactly one line to stdout: the
shell-appropriate `export AWS_PROFILE=...` statement (or `set -gx` for fish,
`$env:AWS_PROFILE = ...` for PowerShell). Everything else - the interactive
picker, SSO login progress/URLs, the resolved account/ARN confirmation, and
all errors - goes to stderr. `awsprof list` and `awsprof whoami` write their
normal (non-activation) output to stdout as usual. The CLI exits non-zero on
error. Prefer explicit subcommands (`list`, `whoami`, `<profile>`) in scripts;
the bare interactive picker is for terminal use only.

## Notes for AI agents

- This project was seeded from an unrelated Bitbucket CLI (`bb`) as a template.
  It has no Bitbucket functionality; ignore any residual references.
- No `aws` CLI dependency: SSO login and identity checks go through
  `aws-sdk-go-v2` directly. The SSO token cache written to
  `~/.aws/sso/cache/*.json` is aws-CLI-compatible, so a subsequent `aws sts
  get-caller-identity` reuses the same cached token.
- Activation requires the shell hook (see "Required setup" above) to be
  installed in the invoking shell; running the bare binary without the hook
  only prints the export line and cannot change the caller's environment.
